trigger_note: Step 11.5 not part of original epoch 12 v5 pipeline. Run retrospectively under v7 adversarial protocol per Phase 2 of v7 build instructions.
stripping_rule_applied: Removed all source-domain musical-metaphor anchors: 'PTCH', 'Pan-Tuned Concept Heads', 'steel pan', 'oil drum', 'note', 'note section', 'tuner', 'groove', 'overtones', 'fundamental' (as musical term — replaced with 'fundamental magnitude'), 'harmonic-alignment loss' (renamed 'integer-ratio alignment loss'), 'each head becomes a note'. Preserved LLM artefacts: multi-head attention, top-K singular directions, integer-ratio (1:2:3:...) constraint, SVD, weight matrix, LoRA, alignment loss, orthogonality regularization.
stripped_llm_application: A multi-head attention fine-tuning rule that constrains each head's top-K singular directions to integer-ratio (1:2:3:...) multiples of a per-head fundamental magnitude in the embedding space. The constraint is enforced by an explicit alignment loss penalizing deviation from the integer-ratio target spectrum on per-head SVD magnitudes. Inter-head orthogonality regularization is also applied. The mechanism operates on transformer attention-head weight SVD (or per-head LoRA update SVD), constraining the top-K singular value magnitudes within each head to obey integer ratios.
sub_mechanisms: M_1: Per-attention-head SVD decomposition of weight matrix (or LoRA update)
M_2: Selection of top-K singular directions within each head
M_3: Integer-ratio (1:2:3:...) target spectrum constraint on singular-value magnitudes within each head
M_4: Explicit alignment loss penalizing deviation from integer-ratio target

raw_skeptical_output.candidate_summary_stripped_of_source: A fine-tuning method that performs per-attention-head SVD on weight matrices (or LoRA updates), constrains the top-K singular values to follow an integer-ratio (1:2:3:...) target spectrum via an explicit alignment loss, and adds inter-head orthogonality regularization. The mechanism shapes the spectral magnitudes of each head while enforcing head diversity.

papers_found.title: SVFT: Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning with Singular Vectors
papers_found.justification: Per-weight-matrix SVD with structured constraint on update coefficients along singular directions; covers M_1, M_2, partial M_3/M_4 but not integer-ratio per se.

papers_found.title: SALT: Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning via Singular Value Adaptation
papers_found.justification: Explicit scale/shift transformations applied to top singular values of weight matrices, functionally equivalent to spectral magnitude shaping with target distribution.

papers_found.title: SORSA / SODA: Spectral Orthogonal Decomposition Adaptation
papers_found.justification: Combines spectral (SVD) decomposition adaptation with orthogonality regularization in fine-tuning, covering M_1, M_2, M_5 and the alignment-loss machinery of M_4 directly; only M_3's specific integer-ratio target differs.

papers_found.title: Spectral Adapter: Fine-Tuning in Spectral Space
papers_found.justification: Operates explicitly in SVD spectral space on per-layer weights with rank-K selection and adapter constraints; the apparatus of shaping top-K spectrum is present, integer-ratio target is the only novel piece.

papers_found.title: CLoRA: Controlled Low-Rank Adaptation with Subspace Constraints
papers_found.justification: Applies orthogonal loss to LoRA A/B factors on multi-head attention v-proj per layer, jointly enforcing subspace/orthogonality constraints; covers M_1, M_2, M_4, M_5 explicitly.

raw_skeptical_output.skeptical_assessment: The candidate's overall scaffold (per-head SVD on attention weights / LoRA updates, top-K spectrum shaping via an alignment loss, inter-head orthogonality regularization) is densely covered by 2024-2025 literature (SVFT, SALT, SORSA/SODA, Spectral Adapter, CLoRA). The only genuinely distinctive element is the specific choice of an integer-ratio (1:2:3:...) target spectrum, which appears to be a particular parametrization of an already-published spectral-shaping family rather than a new mechanism. Without a strong empirical or theoretical justification that integer ratios outperform learned/exponential/uniform spectra, this is best framed as a minor variant of the existing spectral-shaping + orthogonality-regularized PEFT paradigm.

top_paper.title: SORSA / SODA: Spectral Orthogonal Decomposition Adaptation

verdict_change_from_prior_audits: DOWNGRADE: prior triple-audit verdict was UNCERTAIN (HONEST PASS with caveat). v7 adversarial protocol downgrades to FAIL_ADVERSARIAL.
verdict_rationale: Five 2024-2025 papers (SORSA/SODA at 0.80, CLoRA at 0.78, SVFT at 0.75, SALT at 0.72, Spectral Adapter at 0.70) jointly cover M_1, M_2, M_4, M_5 of the PTCH kernel. M_3 (integer-ratio 1:2:3 target spectrum) is the only sub-mechanism NOT directly covered. The skeptical-reviewer judged that M_3 is a parametrization choice within an already-published spectral-shaping family rather than an independent novelty axis. Under the v7 adversarial threshold (max_functional_similarity >= 0.7), this constitutes adversarial_hit = true.
interpretation_note: Prior R279 audits (output/r279_audit.md, output/r279_r302_audit.md, output/r279_final_audit.md) maintained UNCERTAIN status because no SINGLE paper achieved a >=0.7 hit specifically on the integer-ratio + within-head SVD combination. The v7 adversarial protocol is stricter: the skeptical-reviewer is empowered to judge functional similarity on the OVERALL scaffold (M_1 + M_2 + M_4 + M_5) and discount M_3 as a parametrization choice. This is the v7 design intent: when the scaffolding is published, the candidate is not novel even if one parametrization detail is unique.
